extraction.one_line_summary: KÄMNA Lennard attacked from 14-rider break on Etna's lower slopes, caught OLDANI Stefano who went early, held off LÓPEZ Juan Pedro for stage win while López took maglia rosa.
tactical_patterns: OLDANI attacked at climb base for bonus seconds while protecting teammate's jersey—risky dual objective failed when caught.
Trek-Segafredo abandoned MOLLEMA's pink defense early, sending him to gruppetto to preserve energy for later stages.
KÄMNA and LÓPEZ Juan Pedro appeared to negotiate stage-for-jersey inside 3km, both sprinting anyway shows breakaway diplomacy unreliable.
Bahrain-Victorious placed three riders in GC group plus KÄMNA forward—depth overwhelmed single-leader teams on first MTF.

rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: timed final acceleration perfectly on Etna's upper slopes, showing grand tour stage-winning form carries between events
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: took maglia rosa on Giro debut by placing from break, revealing unexpected GC capability for Trek
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: first GC name distanced with 15km remaining on moderate Etna gradient signals poor early form
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: distanced with 5km to summit on home island mountain confirms complete absence of competitive level
rider_signals.OLDANI Stefano: neutral: attacked too early from break base despite defending teammate's jersey—misjudged winning distance
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: attempted final-meters acceleration for bonus seconds shows aggressive racing mentality despite grouped GC finish
